# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.224.0/http/server.ts";

type PluginModule = {
  default?: unknown;
  handler?: (req: Request, ctx: PluginContext) => Response | Promise<Response>;
  fetch?: (req: Request, ctx: PluginContext) => Response | Promise<Response>;
  routes?: Record<string, (req: Request, ctx: PluginContext) => Response | Promise<Response>>;
  name?: string;
  version?: string;
};

type PluginContext = {
  url: URL;
  env: Record<string, string>;
  pluginId: string;
  pluginSpec: string;
  pluginUrl: string;
  now: () => number;
};

const MAX_PLUGINS = 10;
const MAX_SPEC_LEN = 2048;

function getEnvRecord(): Record<string, string> {
  const out: Record<string, string> = {};
  try {
    for (const [k, v] of Deno.env.toObject ? Object.entries(Deno.env.toObject()) : []) out[k] = v;
  } catch {
    // Deno Deploy may restrict env access depending on configuration.
  }
  return out;
}

function splitSpecs(raw: string | null): string[] {
  if (!raw) return [];
  return raw
    .split(/[,\n]/g)
    .map((s) => s.trim())
    .filter(Boolean)
    .slice(0, MAX_PLUGINS);
}

function normalizeSpec(spec: string): string {
  const s = spec.trim();
  if (!s) throw new Error("Empty module spec");
  if (s.length > MAX_SPEC_LEN) throw new Error("Module spec too long");
  return s;
}

function resolveToUrl(spec: string, base: URL): string {
  const s = normalizeSpec(spec);

  // Allow absolute http(s) URLs and relative paths resolved against request origin.
  if (/^https?:\/\//i.test(s)) return s;

  // Allow "npm:" and "jsr:" specifiers (Deploy supports these depending on runtime).
  if (/^(npm:|jsr:)/i.test(s)) return s;

  // Allow absolute path-like "/plugins/x.ts" resolved to same origin.
  if (s.startsWith("/")) return new URL(s, base).toString();

  // Allow relative "./plugins/x.ts" resolved to same origin.
  if (s.startsWith("./") || s.startsWith("../")) return new URL(s, base).toString();

  // Otherwise treat as a bare specifier (may work if import maps are configured).
  return s;
}

function safePluginId(spec: string): string {
  const cleaned = spec.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._:@/-]+/g, "_");
  return cleaned.length > 80 ? cleaned.slice(0, 80) : cleaned;
}

const moduleCache = new Map<string, Promise<PluginModule>>();

async function loadPlugin(pluginUrl: string): Promise<PluginModule> {
  let p = moduleCache.get(pluginUrl);
  if (!p) {
    p = import(pluginUrl) as Promise<PluginModule>;
    moduleCache.set(pluginUrl, p);
  }
  return await p;
}

function json(data: unknown, init: ResponseInit = {}): Response {
  const headers = new Headers(init.headers);
  if (!headers.has("content-type")) headers.set("content-type", "application/json; charset=utf-8");
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2), { ...init, headers });
}

function text(data: string, init: ResponseInit = {}): Response {
  const headers = new Headers(init.headers);
  if (!headers.has("content-type")) headers.set("content-type", "text/plain; charset=utf-8");
  return new Response(data, { ...init, headers });
}

function getPluginSpecs(reqUrl: URL): string[] {
  const qp = reqUrl.searchParams.getAll("module").flatMap((v) => splitSpecs(v));
  const qpSingle = splitSpecs(reqUrl.searchParams.get("modules"));
  const env = getEnvRecord();
  const envSpecs = splitSpecs(env.PLUGINS ?? env.PLUGIN_MODULES ?? env.MODULES ?? null);

  const merged = [...qp, ...qpSingle, ...envSpecs].map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean);
  const unique: string[] = [];
  const seen = new Set<string>();
  for (const s of merged) {
    if (seen.has(s)) continue;
    seen.add(s);
    unique.push(s);
    if (unique.length >= MAX_PLUGINS) break;
  }
  return unique;
}

async function runPlugin(mod: PluginModule, req: Request, ctx: PluginContext): Promise<Response | null> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const routeKey = url.pathname;

  if (mod.routes && typeof mod.routes === "object") {
    const fn = mod.routes[routeKey];
    if (typeof fn === "function") return await fn(req, ctx);
  }

  const handler = (typeof mod.fetch === "function" ? mod.fetch : undefined) ??
    (typeof mod.handler === "function" ? mod.handler : undefined);

  if (handler) return await handler(req, ctx);

  if (typeof mod.default === "function") {
    const fn = mod.default as (req: Request, ctx: PluginContext) => Response | Promise<Response>;
    return await fn(req, ctx);
  }

  return null;
}

serve(async (req: Request) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);

  if (url.pathname === "/health") return text("ok");

  const specs = getPluginSpecs(url);
  const env = getEnvRecord();

  if (url.pathname === "/" && specs.length === 0) {
    return json({
      ok: true,
      message: "No plugins configured. Provide ?module=... or env PLUGINS/PLUGIN_MODULES.",
      usage: {
        query: ["?module=https://example.com/plugin.ts", "?modules=a.ts,b.ts", "?module=jsr:@scope/pkg"],
        env: ["PLUGINS=https://example.com/plugin.ts,https://example.com/other.ts"],
      },
      limits: { maxPlugins: MAX_PLUGINS, maxSpecLen: MAX_SPEC_LEN },
    });
  }

  const results: Array<{
    spec: string;
    pluginUrl?: string;
    pluginId?: string;
    loaded?: boolean;
    error?: string;
    handled?: boolean;
    status?: number;
  }> = [];

  for (const spec of specs) {
    const pluginId = safePluginId(spec);
    let pluginUrl: string;
    try {
      pluginUrl = resolveToUrl(spec, url);
    } catch (e) {
      results.push({ spec, pluginId, loaded: false, error: String(e?.message ?? e) });
      continue;
    }

    try {
      const mod = await loadPlugin(pluginUrl);
      const ctx: PluginContext = {
        url,
        env,
        pluginId,
        pluginSpec: spec,
        pluginUrl,
        now: () => Date.now(),
      };

      const resp = await runPlugin(mod, req, ctx);
      if (resp) {
        const headers = new Headers(resp.headers);
        headers.set("x-plugin-id", pluginId);
        headers.set("x-plugin-url", pluginUrl);
        return new Response(resp.body, { status: resp.status, statusText: resp.statusText, headers });
      }

      results.push({ spec, pluginUrl, pluginId, loaded: true, handled: false });
    } catch (e) {
      results.push({ spec, pluginUrl, pluginId, loaded: false, error: String(e?.message ?? e) });
    }
  }

  if (url.pathname === "/_plugins") {
    return json({
      ok: true,
      configured: specs.length,
      results,
      note:
        "Plugins can export handler(req, ctx), fetch(req, ctx), default(req, ctx), or routes{[pathname]: fn}. First plugin that returns a Response wins.",
    });
  }

  return json(
    {
      ok: false,
      message: "No plugin handled the request.",
      path: url.pathname,
      configured: specs.length,
      results,
      hint: "Visit /_plugins to see plugin load results. Provide plugins via ?module=... or env PLUGINS.",
    },
    { status: 404 },
  );
});
